memory.summary: I have a free afternoon and could easily slack off on leisure, but that project is barely started and due Thursday. I need to kill this quick chore now before I get distracted.
That 30-minute win barely moved the needle on a massive project, proving my quick fixes are useless. I need to stop treating this like a game and commit to real work now.
That instant win feels hollow; the project is still stalled while my afternoon vanishes. I need to stop chasing trivial progress and commit to deep work now.

goals.description: Hard stop the grind immediately. I've hit 0.98; pushing for perfection now is just ego-driven procrastination that will crash my stamina by tomorrow. Sleep is the only way to actually finish this thing.
goals.rationale: My impulsive axis screams 'just one more fix,' but my achievement values demand a functional output tomorrow, not a burnt-out mess today. I need to assertively cut off the dopamine trap of the trivial chore and prioritize rest so I can execute at full capacity later.

goals.description: Send a brief, direct text to cohort_3 stating I'm offline but on it, then physically put the phone away. No guilt-tripping myself with 'I'll talk later' when I have zero energy.
goals.rationale: I don't have the bandwidth for deep social connection right now, but disappearing violates my baseline trust with my close friend group. A quick update satisfies their need without draining my remaining focus. It's efficient and honest.

agent.last_reasoning: Goal g1 demands I stop the ego-driven grind now. Pushing further burns stamina needed for tomorrow's deadline. Sleep restores energy to execute properly later, outweighing the trivial chore's completion.
